# MCP Skills

> Trust-score any AI skill or MCP server. 12 signals across 4 dimensions with safety scanning for prompt injection, credential theft, and supply chain attacks.

## Docs

- [Trust Scanner](https://mcpskills.io): Paste any GitHub repo URL and get a trust score across 12 signals grouped into 4 dimensions (Alive, Legit, Solid, Usable) with trust tier assignment.
- [How Trust Scoring Works](https://mcpskills.io#methodology): Explains the 4-dimension scoring framework, what each dimension measures, and how Skills Mode activates enhanced safety scanning for AI skills.
- [Pricing](https://mcpskills.io#pricing): Three tiers — free scans show trust tier and dimension scores, $9 full reports unlock all 12 signals and safety findings, $29 for a 10-pack.
- [Privacy Policy](https://mcpskills.io/privacy): What data is collected during scans, third-party services used (GitHub API, OpenSSF, LemonSqueezy, Netlify), and data retention policy.
- [Terms of Service](https://mcpskills.io/terms): Acceptable use, no security guarantee disclaimer, intellectual property, and refund policy for paid reports.

## Blog

- [State of AI Skill Security — March 2026](https://mcpskills.io/blog/state-of-ai-skill-security): Original research scoring 10 popular AI tools and developer libraries. Findings include stars not predicting trust, usability as the universal weak spot, and org-backed repos scoring higher on credibility.
- [ClawHavoc and the Missing Trust Layer](https://mcpskills.io/blog/clawhavoc-missing-trust-layer): Analysis of the ClawHavoc attack (1,184 malicious AI skills) and why the AI skill ecosystem needs multi-dimensional trust scoring beyond binary malware detection.
- [How to Check if an AI Skill is Safe Before Installing](https://mcpskills.io/blog/how-to-check-ai-skill-safe): Step-by-step guide to evaluating AI skill safety using trust scoring, dimension analysis, and safety scan interpretation.

## Tools

- [MCP Server (@mcpskillsio/server)](https://github.com/bebravebekind/mcpskills-server): Install via `npx @mcpskillsio/server` to get trust scores inside Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP client. Three tools: check_trust_score, scan_safety, list_packages.

## Key Facts

- Built by Michael Browne at Rise Above Partners LLC
- Headquartered in Viroqua, Wisconsin
- Scores repos across 4 dimensions: Alive, Legit, Solid, Usable
- 12 trust signals derived from GitHub API and OpenSSF Scorecard data
- Skills Mode activates for AI skills and MCP servers with 5 additional safety scans
- Safety scans cover: prompt injection, shell execution, credential access, network exfiltration, obfuscated payloads
- Scan patterns based on ClawHavoc and ToxicSkills attack research
- Free tier: unlimited scans with trust tier and dimension scores
- Paid reports: $9 single or $29 for 10-pack with full signal breakdown
- Launched March 2026
